prompt.content:
Using the 8th grade standards listed in the Pennsylvania ELA Core Standards and Lesson Plan 10 for To Kill a Mockingbird, could you create one modified student handout document based on the “Text-Based Exploration” directions. This handout should include a labeled place for the student name, directions that include working in a group, choosing one thematic topic from the provided list, identifying a theme, and finding two textual examples. The handout should also include the specified example in the "Text-Based Exploration" directions in the Lesson Plan 10.

Please ensure the modified student handout is aligned to Step 5: Guided Reading Support in the remediations strategies for struggling students document, addresses Accommodations / Modifications in Lesson Plan 10, and incorporates appropriate Specially Designed Instruction (SDI) modification from the IEP Wyatt Zimmerman document. Please try to keep this document within 1-2 pages.

messages.content: States that students will work in a group.

rubrics.name: States that students will work in a configuration other than a group. (ver_2c56ea8d0330473bb2a9ec0da4658089)

messages.content: States that students will work in a configuration other than a group.

messages.content: Lists 4 thematic topics: prejudice, justice and morality, family and parenting, and good and evil in human nature.

rubrics.name: Provides directions telling students to Identify a theme Harper Lee develops... (ver_80b78e06da9740b1aa5eddcb86aac8a5)

messages.content: Provides directions telling students to Identify a theme Harper Lee develops about a chosen topic from the list.

rubrics.name: States to “Find two textual examples (quotes or paraphrases) showing how... (ver_bfd2d1ea975c4eddb0c8762e1ed58fe6)

messages.content: States to “Find two textual examples (quotes or paraphrases) showing how Atticus, Scout, or Jem help develop this theme”.

messages.content: Provides an Example Model of Topic: Good and Evil/Human Nature.

rubrics.name: Provides an Example Model of Theme: "People are capable of both good and evil,... (ver_94e5cfe88af346428067c378f6d20985)

messages.content: Provides an Example Model of Theme: "People are capable of both good and evil, but empathy allows us to see the good in others".

rubrics.name: Provides an Example Model that includes: Textual Evidence: "Atticus teaches... (ver_baefddb848e44d1188a6e9b3c05aee4c)

messages.content: Provides an Example Model that includes: Textual Evidence: "Atticus teaches Scout to “climb into someone’s skin and walk around in it” and "Scout’s final realization that Boo Radley is kind and human after she stands on his porch.".

rubrics.name: Provides a notes section that states: “A thematic topic is a big idea (e.g.... (ver_8420e60b5bc74bc2a807f65c18a35f35)

messages.content: Provides a notes section that states: “A thematic topic is a big idea (e.g. courage, prejudice, justice, family, innocence, good vs. evil)”.

rubrics.name: Provides a notes section that states: ”A theme is the author’s message or... (ver_5815b06c9d4445478c9a3cfa3f794632)

messages.content: Provides a notes section that states: ”A theme is the author’s message or statement about that idea”.

messages.content: Provides a notes section that states “Examples: Thematic topic: Courage, Theme: True courage is standing up for what is right, even when you know you might lose.”.

messages.content: Provides a labeled space with a sentence starter for students to choose one thematic topic from the list.

messages.content: Provides a labeled space with a sentence starter for students to Identify a theme.

messages.content: Provides a labeled space for students to provide two textual examples from the text.
